DMSE.MissileBodyDef.label: light missile body
DMSE.MissileBodyDef.description: A compact single-stage missile chassis with three assembly slots: warhead, guidance, and payload. Faster and longer-ranged than a loitering munition, but lighter and cheaper than a full cruise missile.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: light HE warhead
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: A compact high-explosive warhead scaled for light missiles. Standard blast, no special effects.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: incendiary warhead
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: Scatters burning thermite on impact. Ignites all cells within N radius regardless of line of sight.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: continuous rod warhead
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: A ring of steel rods that expand on detonation, maximising fragment density against aerial targets. Provides an intercept hit-chance bonus when used as a SAM round.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: inertial guidance
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: Baseline strap-down inertial reference unit. No seekers; relies on pre-programmed flight path. Scatter unchanged.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: infrared seeker
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: A passive infrared seeker that homes on body heat. Targets the nearest colonist if any are present; falls back to inertial targeting otherwise.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: anti-radiation seeker
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: Homes on active radar emissions. Prioritises the most powerful search or fire-control radar on the target map, then falls back to high-energy targeting.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: no payload
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: No secondary payload. The missile relies entirely on its primary warhead detonation.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: HE payload
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: A secondary high-explosive charge that detonates after the primary warhead, extending the damage radius.

DMSE.MissilePartDef.label: toxic gas payload
DMSE.MissilePartDef.description: Disperses a cloud of toxic gas across N tiles, persisting long after the blast to deny area to unprotected infantry.
